PostSnail
Admin Forest Manifesto Docs Media Kit

Creator and developer docs

PostSnail Documentation

Everything needed to write a microblog, publish it as static files, register it in Forest, and understand the proof architecture.

How To Use PostSnailCreate identity, write posts, attach images, export encrypted workspaces, and download a public Website ZIP. Encrypted Workspace VaultUse private .postsnail files as the editable source for your microblog. Publish On Cloudflare PagesUpload the public Website ZIP contents and verify public proof files. PostSnail ConceptThe local-first publishing model, Forest tracker role, and trust boundaries. PostSnail ArchitectureAdmin modules, static bundle shape, proof files, registry Worker, and extension points. Security NotesWhat workspace encryption protects, what proof files prove, and what passphrase loss means. Compatibility ContractStable core, optional extensions, required features, legacy warnings, and migration promises. Protocol ReferenceManifest, well-known identity, commit proofs, Shell vaults, and tracker announce records. Workspace MigrationsHow versioned workspace migrations keep old editable projects importable. PSEP ProcessHow PostSnail Enhancement Proposals govern protocol-risk changes. PostSnail LegalApache-2.0 license, NOTICE attribution, third-party notices, logo provenance, and Forest cost notes.

© 2026 Boaz Alhadeff. PostSnail is Apache-2.0 licensed; redistributed copies must preserve NOTICE attribution. See Legal and Third-party notices.
